audience.primary_audience: Austin homeowners aged 35-60 facing roof damage, aging roofs, or post-storm repairs — likely comparing 2-3 contractors and nervous about being overcharged or underserved
customer_pains: Worried their roof damage will get worse or cause interior water damage if they wait too long
Burned by or scared of shady contractors who overpromise, underdeliver, and disappear after the job
customer_desires: A trustworthy local roofer who shows up, communicates clearly, and gets the job done right the first time
Peace of mind that their home is protected and their investment is backed by a real guarantee
customer_objections: How do I know this company is better than the other Austin roofers I've seen?
This is a big investment — what happens if something goes wrong after the job?

brand_direction.recommended_tone: Confident, direct, and locally grounded — the tone of a seasoned Austin contractor who doesn't need to hard-sell because the work speaks for itself. Warm but urgent. Trustworthy but not boring.
brand_direction.offer_angle: Position as Austin's most accountable roofing company — lead with a risk-reversal offer like a free inspection or satisfaction guarantee, stack the value with local proof, and frame every interaction as stress-free and homeowner-first

conversion_strategy.primary_cta: Get Your Free Roof Inspection — No Pressure, No Obligation. We'll come to you.
trust_elements_needed: Austin-specific testimonials referencing neighborhoods, storm events, or named projects to signal deep local roots and real accountability
A visible guarantee or risk-reversal badge — free inspection, workmanship warranty, or satisfaction promise — placed near every CTA to disarm high-ticket hesitation
recommended_sections: Pain-led opener that calls out the exact moment homeowners find themselves on this page — storm damage, aging shingles, a leak they've been ignoring — then immediately bridges to the solution
Before and after project gallery organized by roof type or neighborhood to build visual proof and help homeowners self-identify with past customers
A simple 3-step process section showing how easy it is to get started — reducing friction and making the decision feel low-risk and manageable

top_hooks: That water stain on your ceiling isn't going away on its own — here's how Austin homeowners are protecting their homes before the next storm hits
Most Austin roofers will take your deposit and leave you guessing. Here's what working with a contractor who actually answers the phone looks like.
top_benefits: Licensed Austin roofers who know local weather patterns, HOA requirements, and the exact materials that hold up in Central Texas heat
Every job backed by a written workmanship guarantee — so you're not just paying for a new roof, you're paying for permanent peace of mind
top_problems_to_call_out: A damaged or aging roof that's quietly driving up energy bills and threatening everything underneath it
The paralysis of not knowing who to trust when every roofing company in Austin makes the exact same promises

assumptions: Austin Roofing Company serves residential homeowners as its primary customer segment based on landing page framing and offer structure
The business operates across greater Austin and surrounding areas but has not yet leveraged neighborhood-level or storm-event-specific social proof in its copy
The company likely has a portfolio of completed jobs but has not yet formatted them into a visual before-and-after gallery for the website
A free inspection or consultation offer is either already in place or is a realistic lead-gen mechanism the business could deploy immediately given industry norms
The business competes primarily on trust and differentiation rather than price, based on the value-stack, offer-led page structure identified in the analysis
